title: 10 best herbal creams for cough

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with cough because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated throat tissues, reducing discomfort and pain. The herbal extract also contains compounds that boost the immune system, helping to fight off underlying infections that cause coughing. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea creams may help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and provide rapid relief from coughing fits, allowing for a faster recovery from respiratory illnesses. This natural remedy is often used to combat colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and promote the recovery of the immune system, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.

Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity of cough by inhibiting the replication of viruses that cause respiratory infections and by reducing inflammation in the airways.

Polysaccharides: These compounds have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to enhance the immune system's response to infections, reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm the throat.
Roots: Utilized for their immunomodulatory effects to help boost the immune system and fight off infections.
Leaves: Employed for their antimicrobial properties to help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a clean area of a garden.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil like coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix in 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Whip the mixture in a double boiler until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency taking about 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with cough because they contain thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, thymol can help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce congestion. The cream's anti-inflammatory effects can also help to ease coughing by reducing inflammation in the airways. Additionally, thymol's antimicrobial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief. This can provide quick relief from coughing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, thereby relieving cough symptoms.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits antitussive properties, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs by acting on the central nervous system and suppressing cough reflexes.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used due to their high content of thymol, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make cough creams because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are utilized for cough creams due to their rich content of thymol and other essential oils, which aid in relieving congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Steep the leaves in 100 milliliters of jojoba oil for 2 hours to infuse the oil with herbal essence.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth to remove the solids and extract the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 200 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of beeswax to create the cream base.
Whip the cream base until it's smooth and creamy then transfer it to an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with cough because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to keep the throat hydrated, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm. Additionally, glycyrrhizin's ability to thin mucus helps to relieve coughing fits and make breathing easier. As a result, glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with cough by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and acting as a natural expectorant, loosening mucus and making it easier to cough up.

Flavonoids (including isoquercetin and astragalin): They help with cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe the mucous membranes and reduce irritation in the throat and airways.

Saponins (including glycyrrhetol and glycyrrhetic acid): They help with cough by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the airways, as well as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections that may be contributing to the cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are the most commonly used part for cough creams due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to soothe and calm coughs.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, although in smaller quantities, and can add to the overall soothing and anti-inflammatory effects of the cough cream.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500ml of carrier oil at 70-80°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove roots and sediment.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler to melt at 180-190°f.
Add 50ml of the infused oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into clean containers and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with cough because they contain the essential oil eucalyptol, which has natural decongestant properties. When applied topically, eucalyptol helps to break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion. The menthol content in these creams also helps to calm the throat and reduce irritation, providing quick relief from coughing fits. As a result, eucalyptus globulus creams are often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and colds.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with cough by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
Cineole: It helps with cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and relieving congestion, making it easier to breathe and cough.
Beta-caryophyllene: It helps with cough by having anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, reducing swelling and pain in the throat and airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oil, which contains eucalyptol, acts as a decongestant and helps to relieve cough.
Barks: They are rich in tannins and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to ease cough and congestion.
Stems: The essential oils extracted from the stems contain compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to thin mucus and provide relief from cough.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 5g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the infused oil and heat gently until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a mold and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The menthol content in the cream helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and throat. This provides instant relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, mentha's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the irritated tissues in the throat and chest, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with a persistent cough. The cream's cooling sensation also calms the cough reflex, providing rapid relief from coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps with cough by creating a cooling sensation in the throat, reducing inflammation and temporarily numbing the nerve endings to relieve irritation and discomfort.
Menthone: It helps with cough by relaxing the muscles in the throat and reducing spasms, making it easier to cough up mucus and other irritants.
Eugenol: It helps with cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, reducing inflammation and fighting off infections in the throat that can cause coughing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of menthol, a key compound for its decongestant and analgesic properties.
Buds: They have a higher menthol content than leaves, making them ideal for creating potent cough creams.
Rhyzomes: They contain menthone, a compound that helps to relieve coughs and colds, and is often used in combination with menthol.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 100g of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 50ml of distilled water and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with cough because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Gingerol helps to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and other irritants. The warming and soothing sensation of ginger creams can also provide quick relief from a sore throat and calm an irritated cough, providing fast and effective relief from coughing. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing coughs.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve cough and congestion by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting the removal of mucus.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the severity and frequency of cough.
Gingerols' and Shogaols' precursor, Zingiberene: This terpene has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections that can cause cough, such as the common cold or flu.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Stems: Utilized for their secondary metabolites, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their soothing and decongestant effects.
Buds: Employed for their bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried zingiber officinale roots and 10g of beeswax for the herbal cream mixture.
Infuse 50g of dried zingiber officinale roots in 250ml of carrier oil for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Combine 50g of infused carrier oil with 10g of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer for 2 minutes and 30 seconds.
Pour the final mixture into a 100ml container and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus creams helps with cough because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herb, also known as mullein, has been traditionally used to soothe irritated throats and calm coughs. The creams or ointments are often made by infusing the leaves and flowers of the plant in a carrier oil, which is then applied topically to the throat and chest area. This can help to reduce inflammation and promote the clearance of mucus, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby reducing cough.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Verbascum thapsus have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat underlying infections that may be contributing to cough.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in the plant may help alleviate cough by reducing inflammation and exerting a mild sedative effect on the respiratory system, thereby providing relief from cough and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make creams for cough due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make creams for cough due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the respiratory system.
Roots: The roots of Verbascum thapsus are used to make creams for cough due to their antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections in the respiratory tract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with cough because of its soothing and calming properties. The calming effects of lavender oil have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and lungs, providing relief from coughing spasms. The antiseptic and antimicrobial properties of lavender oil also help to combat infections that can cause coughing. Additionally, the relaxing effects of lavender oil can promote better sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from illness and fight off infections. This makes lavender creams a natural and effective remedy for cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving cough symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, reducing inflammation and soothing the airways to alleviate coughing.
Caryophyllene oxide: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat infections that may cause coughing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used in creams for cough due to their expectorant properties, which help in loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flowers: They are used in creams for cough due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help in soothing the throat and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: They are used in creams for cough due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried lavandula flowers from a sunny area at 6 am to ensure best oil content.
Extract 200ml of oil from lavandula flowers by infusing them in 1l of carrier oil for 6 hours.
Mix 50ml of the extracted lavandula oil with 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter.
Heat the mixture from step 3 in a double boiler at 160f for 10 minutes or until it's smooth.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with cough because it contains quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes irritated throats and reduces coughing. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion. Additionally, quinine has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughs and congestion caused by respiratory infections. This makes Cinchona officinalis creams a popular natural remedy for coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds have a drying effect on the respiratory tract, helping to reduce mucus production and alleviate cough symptoms.
Quinic acids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the irritated tissues in the throat and reduce cough severity.
Flavonoids: These compounds have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their high content of quinine, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory that helps soothe coughs.
Barks: Used for their alkaloid content, particularly quinine, which helps in relieving cough and reduces inflammation.
Stems: Used for their quinine content, which provides a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect to help soothe coughs.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 part of dried cinchona officinalis bark in a double boiler and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean bowl.
Combine the strained liquid with 2 parts of sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture gently and then let it infuse for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with cough because they contain elderberry extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compounds in Sambucus nigra, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract. This leads to a decrease in coughing and an improvement in overall respiratory health. Additionally, Sambucus nigra creams can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing quick relief from coughing and other respiratory symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with cough by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system to alleviate respiratory symptoms.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: They help with cough by exhibiting antitussive (cough-suppressing) and expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory congestion.
Tannins: They help with cough by possessing astringent properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antiviral and antitussive properties, making them effective in soothing coughs.
Leaves: Contain bioactive compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.
Fruits: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Infuse the dried sambucus nigra flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax to create a 20% wax content and a 30% sambucus nigra extract content.
Add a small amount of vitamin e oil as an antioxidant to prevent spoilage and oxidation.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly and pour the mixture into small containers for cooling and solidification.
Label the containers with the date and contents and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cough are topical preparations that combine the soothing and healing properties of herbs to provide relief from coughs. These creams work by moisturizing the throat, reducing inflammation, and easing congestion. The best herbal creams for cough contain a blend of effective herbs such as Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Verbascum thapsus, Lavandula angustifolia, Cinchona officinalis, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, a persistent cough can disrupt daily life, leading to fatigue, sleep deprivation, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting overall well-being.
